questions.question: What happens to insoluble impurities during electrolytic refining?
What is the primary composition of the stable composite developed by IIT Mandi that prevents 2D semiconductors from degrading?
questions.option_1: Boron nitride mixed with epoxy resin
questions.option_3: Carbon nanotubes embedded in polymer matrix

questions.question: When dilute hydrochloric acid reacts with calcium carbonate, the gas evolved is passed through limewater. On passing the gas in excess, the milkiness disappears due to the formation of:
When magnesium reacts with oxygen to form magnesium oxide, which of the following statements is NOT correct?
questions.option_1: The compound formed contains ionic bonds.
questions.option_2: Magnesium loses electrons to form Mg3+ ions.
questions.option_3: Oxygen gains electrons to form O2- ions.
questions.option_4: Magnesium loses electrons to form Mg2+ ions.

questions.question: When methane (CH₄) burns completely in the presence of sufficient oxygen, which products are formed?
When two aqueous solutions react inside a sealed flask and form products, what happens to the total mass of the system?
questions.option_2: It increases due to gas formation

questions.question: Which calculation correctly gives the molecular mass of nitric acid (HNO₃)?
Which change will promote micelle formation in a detergent solution?
questions.option_1: Adding more water to dilute the solution
questions.option_2: Increasing detergent concentration above Critical Micelle Concentration(CMC)
questions.option_3: Lowering the temperature below Kraft point
questions.option_4: Decreasing detergent concentration below Critical Micelle Concentration(CMC)

questions.question: Which element exists as a monoatomic molecule?
Which metal listed below shows the strongest reaction when added to dilute hydrochloric acid?
Which metal releases the most heat when reacting with excess hydrochloric acid?
Which method helps prevent rancidity?
Which observation supports the law of constant proportions for water?
questions.option_1: Hydrogen and oxygen ratio is fixed

questions.question: Which of the following best supports the concept that matter is made-up of particles?
questions.option_1: A drop of ink spreads uniformly in water

questions.question: Which of the following compounds has a formula unit mass of 100 u?
Which of the following does NOT describe a property of ethanol (C₂H₅OH)?
questions.option_1: It is soluble in water in all proportions.
questions.option_2: It is a clear, colourless liquid with a pleasant smell.
questions.option_3: It turns blue litmus paper red.
questions.option_4: It reacts with sodium to release hydrogen gas.

questions.question: Which of the following everyday examples represents a homogeneous mixture?
questions.option_3: Sand mixed uniformly with iron filings

questions.question: Which of the following everyday mixtures is best classified as a suspension?
Which of the following is the correct relationship between the number of carbon and hydrogen atoms in alkenes?
Which of the following metals is NOT refined by electrolytic refining?
Which of the following statements about Plaster of Paris (POP) is CORRECT?
questions.option_1: POP is CaSO4·½H₂O and hardens by reacting with water
questions.option_2: POP is CaSO4·½H₂O and sets slowly without water
questions.option_3: POP is CaSO4·2H2O and sets quickly on mixing with water
questions.option_4: POP is CaSO4·2H2O and is mainly used as a fertilizer

questions.question: Which of the following statements about washing soda is CORRECT?
questions.option_1: Washing soda is chemically sodium carbonate decahydrate (Na2CO3·10H2O)
questions.option_2: Washing soda is acidic in nature
questions.option_3: It is obtained by heating sodium bicarbonate to 100°C
questions.option_4: It does not remove permanent hardness of water

questions.question: Which of the following statements is TRUE for all saturated hydrocarbons?
questions.option_1: They contain at least one double bond.
questions.option_2: They show substitution reactions rather than addition reactions.
questions.option_4: They are generally very reactive due to triple bonds.

questions.question: Which pair of olfactory indicators loses its smell in a basic solution?
Which process is used to convert zinc carbonate ore into zinc oxide before reduction?
